props.content:
## Usage Guidelines

- Use **primary** for the main action!!!
- Use **danger** only for destructive actions
- Always pair danger with a confirmation dialog

- Textarea component stories
- Textarea component stories.

props.title: #85 Update architecture docs and add comprehensive DOCS.md

github.body:
## What

- Updated 18 stale architecture docs to match current source
- Created 13 new architecture docs for previously undocumented files
- Regenerated architecture index with prose overviews
- Fixed README inaccuracies (package names, feature flag priority, comments config, mountStoryboardCore)
- Created comprehensive DOCS.md usage guide (1,484 lines)

## DOCS.md structure

Three-tier organization designed for the future docs site:

**Learn Storyboard** — Tutorial path: first prototype → flows → reading data → overrides → forms → objects → records

**Features** — Project structure, routing, feature flags, body CSS classes, hide mode, inspector, organizing prototypes, viewfinder, external prototypes, canvas, toolbar, comments, template variables, design systems

**Reference** — Toolbar configuration, storyboard.config.json reference, full API tables

## Key design decisions

- Ordered for learning (flows before objects, data before routing)
- Added "What's wired for you" section to ground new users
- Workshop/toolbar-first path mentioned early for non-technical users
- Inspector section added for data debugging workflow
- Sections kept concise — many sections but each is focused
github.bodyHtml:
What

  • Updated 18 stale architecture docs to match current source
  • Created 13 new architecture docs for previously undocumented files
  • Regenerated architecture index with prose overviews
  • Fixed README inaccuracies (package names, feature flag priority, comments config, mountStoryboardCore)
  • Created comprehensive DOCS.md usage guide (1,484 lines)

DOCS.md structure

Three-tier organization designed for the future docs site:

Learn Storyboard — Tutorial path: first prototype → flows → reading data → overrides → forms → objects → records

Features — Project structure, routing, feature flags, body CSS classes, hide mode, inspector, organizing prototypes, viewfinder, external prototypes, canvas, toolbar, comments, template variables, design systems

Reference — Toolbar configuration, storyboard.config.json reference, full API tables

Key design decisions

  • Ordered for learning (flows before objects, data before routing)
  • Added "What's wired for you" section to ground new users
  • Workshop/toolbar-first path mentioned early for non-technical users
  • Inspector section added for data debugging workflow
  • Sections kept concise — many sections but each is focused

props.title: Comment on #494 Growth Life Cycle - Week of March 3, 2026

github.body:
> ⚠️ **Superseded** — This summary was regenerated to include late updates.

### Security Products — AI-Generated Weekly Summary — Week of 2026-03-02

> This summary was automatically generated. Design Manager: please review and edit this comment as needed. When ready, invoke `/post-to-monday-minutes` in this discussion to publish to the Monday Minutes discussion.

### Insights 👀

- **Security product momentum continues with customer-facing capabilities.** The team shipped the rule index page improvements to GA and completed the code quality vision experience for customer testing, demonstrating tangible progress on security tooling.

- **Code coverage architecture decisions are creating complexity.** The team is wrestling with whether code coverage should be independent of CodeQL setup, which has implications for both enablement UX and long-term architecture. [[Figma explorations]](https://www.figma.com/design/8gsIkUVp01HgzoAKa1C1Qb/Code-quality--test-coverage?node-id=45-10411)

- **Automated Review Comments are creating unexpected merge blocking behavior.** The shift to ARC components is surfacing integration challenges with existing branch protection rules, potentially affecting multiple security features and requiring cross-functional coordination. This wasn't anticipated in the original design.

### 🚀 Momentum

#### Ships that moved the needle

- **Rule index page improvements (GA)** make it easier for developers to navigate and understand security rules at scale. @heyamie [[Issue]](https://github.com/github/code-scanning/issues/21460)

- **Code quality vision experience (Staff Ship)** is ready for customer validation, marking a key milestone in the security products roadmap. @heyamie @dfosco [[Experience]](https://github.github.com/code-quality-user-testing/branch--first-look/security/quality/standard-findings?scene=Code%20Quality#filterStatus=Open&filterFindingType=&sortBy=alphabetical&sortOrder=desc&bulkSelectionCount=0)

- **Dependabot Alert Assignees (GA)** enables teams to assign ownership of security alerts, improving accountability and response times. @connienguyen [[Changelog]](https://github.blog/changelog/2026-03-03-dependabot-alert-assignees-are-now-generally-available/)

### Risks

- **Code coverage enablement decisions are blocked on architectural clarity.** The team needs to decide if code coverage should be independent of CodeQL workflow setup for code quality. Without resolution by next week's EPD sync, design delivery timelines are at risk. @tassia-sp [[Questions doc]](https://www.figma.com/design/8gsIkUVp01HgzoAKa1C1Qb/Code-quality--test-coverage?node-id=45-10411&p=f&t=0h6BGXWdXwDlAHtx-11)

- **Automated Review Comments integration is creating unintended merge blocking behavior.** License compliance alerts are being blocked by the "requires conversation resolution" rule even when the feature-specific blocking rule isn't enabled. This affects multiple teams (License compliance, Code quality, Code scanning) and needs a solution before more features adopt ARC. @connienguyen

### 🔥 Demos

- **Code coverage enablement explorations** show different paths for enabling test coverage independent of CodeQL setup, addressing key architectural questions about how these features should interact. @tassia-sp [🎥 Loom](https://www.loom.com/share/c8abcf52851c4260bc0f97add008678a)

![Enablement _ hero](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/5077770d-25c8-4b19-9ac9-ad4c57a07934)
![Enablement _ dialog](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/60ec5fa0-711c-46b1-9211-8da5702f5cbd)
![Enablement _ in progress](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/a7089ebc-2367-4d24-8724-4d9ab4e186ed)

- **License compliance permissions models v2** explores using Enterprise Security Manager to address access gaps, providing reviewers with concrete options for the upcoming experience review. @connienguyen [🎥 Loom](https://www.loom.com/share/1f10fe5c059c493a9b78d912e9cfb875)

- **Code quality prototype** demonstrates the new vision experience that's ready for customer feedback, showing improved navigation and filtering capabilities. @heyamie @dfosco

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/69713f35-a53c-4975-ab23-49f2bae73e59

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/be20a60b-da83-4f5d-9e35-d91435f260d9

### 🎉 Kudos

- @heyamie gave a shoutout to @dfosco for collaborating on the code quality prototype 🤝
- @tassia-sp thanked @durfee for a great reforge walkthrough
- @tassia-sp thanked @maximedegreve and @adrianmg for help with Vercel permissions and getting branch deployments working for reforge 🙏

<!-- design-ops-team-summary-superseded:2026-03-02:security-products -->
